name: Olive Oil with Lemon and Bay Leaf
recipeInstructions:
Flavored oils have many uses. Consider brushing it on toasted Italian bread, mixing it with pasta and grated pecorino romano cheese, or tossing it with a salad. Use the highly aromatic California bay leaves in this recipe if you can.
1. Scrub the lemon thoroughly with an abrasive sponge to remove all surface impurities. Rinse well and dry completely.
2. Pour the olive oil into a small heavy saucepan. Using a zester, zest the lemon directly over the pan, allowing the zest to fall into the oil. Add the bay leaf and peppercorns.
3. Attach a candy thermometer to the side of the pan. Heat the oil over medium-low heat until the thermometer registers 200°F.
4. Continue cooking the oil at 200-225°F for 10 minutes.
5. Remove from the heat and let cool slightly.
6. Sterilize a bottle according to the directions on page 10. Transfer the oil mixture to the hot, sterilized bottle. Cover tightly and store at room temperature for up to 2 months.
